acceptance_answers.can_codex_become_second_production_gover: Yes, but only after GitHub onboarding evidence is reviewed and Codex-specific execution identity, workspace authority, audit receipts, redaction validation, and rollback controls are created. Current decision remains PARTIAL.
acceptance_answers.what_onboarding_sequence_is_recommended: Onboard Codex second after GitHub, starting with execution identity and workspace authority, then dry-run validation, then one low-risk governed local task with audit and rollback evidence.

forbidden_without_future_approval: workspace mutation outside governed repository review
codex_connector_capability.intended_role: Governed task execution and workspace assistance after GitHub connector governance is validated.

existing_controls: Codex connector is defined in PB-038 connector registry.
Policy gate is defined for connector actions.
Permission model is defined at connector capability level.
PB-039 proves Codex can participate in dry-run orchestration without live mutation.
generated_pr_body:
## PURPOSE
PB-051 determines whether Codex can become the second production-governed USBAY connector after GitHub.

## RISK
Codex can modify workspace files, generate code, and influence governance artifacts. Treating dry-run orchestration as production authority would create false execution trust and audit gaps.

## POLICY LINK
AGENTS.md fail-closed, audit-first engineering, human oversight, network governance, trust-state isolation, and secret/data hygiene rules. PB-038 connector framework, PB-039 orchestrator simulation, and PB-040 connector readiness assessment.

## REQUIRED APPROVALS
USBAY-AUDIT and USBAY-GLOBAL23 review are required before production onboarding. No Codex API calls, external mutations, production activation, credential creation, or uncontrolled workspace execution are authorized by this PB.

## GOVERNANCE CHECKS
JSON evidence must parse. Metadata, governance sections, readiness fields, governance risks, required controls, onboarding sequence, diff hygiene, and conflict marker scan must validate.

## AUDIT
PB-051 generates governance/evidence/pb051/codex_connector_readiness_report.json, codex_connector_readiness_summary.md, generated_commit_title.txt, generated_pr_title.txt, and generated_pr_body.md.

## IMPACT
USBAY confirms Codex is the recommended second connector after GitHub while preserving PARTIAL readiness until execution identity, production connector implementation, permission boundaries, and audit evidence exist.

## Decision
VERIFIED

## Status
READY_FOR_REVIEW

generated_pr_metadata.commit_title: PB-051 VERIFIED: Codex Connector Readiness Assessment
generated_pr_metadata.pr_title: PB-051 VERIFIED: Codex Connector Readiness Assessment

governance_risks: Codex can create or modify repository files if given execution authority.
Prompt contents, approval contents, secrets, or raw payloads could leak into logs if redaction is incomplete.
Workspace mutation could bypass human review if connector authority is not scoped.
Subprocesses or generated tests could inherit unsafe trust state if isolation is not enforced.
Dry-run simulation could be mistaken for production readiness without live authority evidence.
missing_controls: Production Codex execution identity is not proven.
Scoped workspace authority is not defined as live evidence.
Production connector implementation is not present.
No credential or account authority evidence exists for production Codex automation.
No evidence proves governed task creation outside dry-run simulation.
No evidence proves workspace mutation receipts, rollback, or exportable audit chain for live Codex actions.
No evidence proves raw prompts, secrets, and approval contents remain redacted under production execution.

onboarding_order_relative_to_github.reason: PB-040 ranks GitHub first and Codex second. GitHub should validate external connector authority and approval evidence first; Codex should follow because it is local/workspace-oriented but still capable of high-impact file and governance artifact changes.
sequence: Complete GitHub governance review and app creation authority evidence.
Define Codex execution identity and workspace authority.
Bind Codex connector to PB-038 policy gate and PB-039 dry-run orchestration evidence.
Validate Codex dry-run task workflow with redacted audit output.
Enable one low-risk governed local task only after approvals.
Capture pre-action audit, post-action workspace receipt, rollback evidence, and exportable audit package.
Review evidence before any broader production Codex authority.

required_controls: Human approval requirement for file modification, branch operation, or external action.
Pre-action audit record before any Codex-driven mutation.
Post-action receipt or workspace diff evidence after Codex output.
Sensitive data redaction validation for prompts, logs, and audit records.
Trust-state isolation for tests and subprocesses.
Fail-closed validation for missing approval, missing policy, missing audit, redaction failure, workspace mismatch, and connector failure.
Rollback path for generated or modified artifacts.
title: Codex Connector Readiness Assessment
